description: Amazon.com Inc operates e-commerce, cloud computing (AWS), digital streaming, and artificial intelligence businesses. AWS is the world's largest cloud provider.
known_facts: AWS is the world's largest cloud computing platform with over 30% market share
Andy Jassy is Amazon's CEO since 2021
Amazon Prime has over 200 million subscribers globally
Amazon is the largest e-commerce company in the United States
AWS generates the majority of Amazon's operating income despite smaller revenue share
Amazon acquired MGM in 2022 and Whole Foods in 2017
Amazon Advertising is a fast-growing multi-billion dollar segment
Amazon operates a third-party marketplace with millions of sellers
known_risks: FTC antitrust lawsuit challenging Amazon's marketplace dominance
Labor relations challenges and unionization efforts at Amazon warehouses
Rising fulfillment and logistics costs pressuring retail margins
Competition from Walmart, Shopify, and international e-commerce players
AWS competition from Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud
Regulatory scrutiny over third-party seller practices
known_strengths: AWS profitability subsidizes investment in retail and logistics
Prime ecosystem creates high-value recurring subscription revenue
Logistics network and last-mile delivery advantage
Advertising business with high margins growing rapidly
AI integration across AWS, Alexa, and retail operations
Scale advantages in procurement, warehousing, and shipping
